title: Market-peak froth
subtitle: Public-data gauges of euphoria and complacency.

indicators.name: Consumer Confidence
indicators.description: Household optimism, which runs hot near market tops. The Conference Board index is licensed, so the University of Michigan sentiment index is the documented free proxy.
indicators.threshold: Triggered at or above the 75th percentile of the full monthly history.
indicators.notes: Sentiment sits at the 0th percentile of its full history — nowhere near the optimism seen at tops. Proxy substitution: the Conference Board confidence index is licensed; Michigan sentiment is a different survey that can diverge from it. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Retail Euphoria
indicators.description: Share of individual investors bullish minus bearish in the weekly AAII survey. Extreme bullishness is a contrarian warning — when everyone is optimistic, the buyers are already in.
indicators.threshold: Triggered when the spread reaches the top decile of the full weekly history (currently +28.3 pp); eased when bears outnumber bulls.
indicators.notes: Bears outnumber bulls (spread -10.9 pp) — retail euphoria is off. This week: bulls 31.4%, bears 42.3%; spread percentile 18 across 2,030 weeks since 1987. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Manager Bullishness
indicators.description: Active money managers' reported average equity exposure on a 0-200 scale. Near 100 means managers are all-in with little cash left to deploy — crowded positioning.
indicators.notes: Active managers report 85% average equity exposure, below the 90 all-in line. As-of stamped at collection time; NAAIM posts the number weekly without a machine-readable date. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Growth-Expectation Froth
indicators.description: How many dollars investors pay per dollar of earnings. A high historical percentile means optimism is already priced. Forward P/E needs a vendor feed, so the trailing multiple from multpl is the documented free proxy.
indicators.threshold: Triggered at or above the 90th percentile of monthly history since 1871.
indicators.notes: The trailing multiple sits at the 98th percentile of monthly history since 1871 — a lot of optimism is already priced. Proxy substitution: trailing, not forward, earnings. Recent months in the calibration history are multpl estimates pending final earnings (marked on their table). Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Deal & IPO Froth
indicators.description: New-listing volume clusters at tops, when cheap financing and high confidence embolden dealmakers. US IPO proceeds from Renaissance Capital serve as the documented free proxy for broader issuance.
indicators.threshold: Triggered when the annualised year-to-date proceeds pace reaches the 80th percentile of the prior years available (from 2016).
indicators.notes: Annualised issuance pace ranks at the 100th percentile of the prior years available — deal-making at cycle highs. Year to date: $114.7bn across 6 months (82 IPOs); annualised $229bn. History window is short (10 prior years) — a coarse gauge, flagged per SPEC. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Rule of 20
indicators.description: Trailing P/E plus year-on-year CPI inflation. The old rule of thumb calls 20 fair value; readings far above it mark expensive markets, especially when inflation is not the driver.
indicators.threshold: Triggered above 20 and at or above the 80th percentile of monthly history since 1948.
indicators.notes: P/E plus inflation totals 36.3, well above the 20 fair-value line and at the 97th percentile of history since 1948. Inputs: trailing P/E 32.15 (multpl), CPI +4.2% YoY (FRED CPIAUCSL, cross-checked at the BLS API). Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Value vs Growth
indicators.description: Six-month price-return gap between S&P 500 pure growth (RPG) and pure value (RPV). Sustained growth leadership marks froth-on speculation; value leadership eases it.
indicators.threshold: Triggered when growth leads by 10 pp or more over 126 trading sessions (about six months); eased when value leads.
indicators.notes: Growth leads value by +15.4 pp over six months — the crowding pattern of froth-on leadership. Price returns, not total returns: RPV distributes a materially higher yield, so the spread slightly flatters growth (well inside the trigger margin). Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Credit Complacency
indicators.description: The Chicago Fed's weekly composite of financial conditions; negative is looser than average. Deeply loose conditions signal complacent risk appetite — the fuel of late-cycle froth.
indicators.threshold: Triggered when conditions are in the loosest 20% of weekly readings since 1971.
indicators.notes: Financial conditions (-0.50) sit at the 38th loosest percentile since 1971, outside the complacent tail. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).

indicators.name: Tightening Credit
indicators.description: Net share of banks tightening commercial-loan standards in the Fed's quarterly Senior Loan Officer Survey. A froth-off read: tightening credit withdraws the fuel. Context only — it does not feed the composite.
indicators.threshold: Context row — net tightening (above zero) is a froth-off pressure note.
indicators.notes: A net +8.1% of banks are tightening standards for large and mid-sized firms — froth-off pressure building in credit supply. Series identity verified against two sources (VERIFICATION.md).
